description.en-GB: This display shows menus, EQ curves, and more.
This is the primary way to interact with the L12's menu system.
This is used to modify the delay time on the "Echo" effect on channels one and two.
This is used to modify the wet signal from the "Reverb" effect on channels one and two.
This knob is used to search through various effects, click the knob to select the highlighted effect.
This knob is used to adjust the levels of "bass" and "treble" on the master channel.
These knobs are used to control the gain signal level on the input of channels one through three.
These knobs are used to control the output level of channels four and five.
These buttons are used to navigate and play files off a USB stick or Bluetooth-connected device.
This button is used for pairing one L12 with another L12.
This LED will light up when the L12 is limiting the output, to mitigate distortion and protect itself.
These LEDs will light up when an audio source is providing audio at an adequate level on the corresponding channel.
This USB port is for charging devices.
This USB port is used for playing media off a USB stick or similar media.
This "passes through" the master audio signal so you can daisy chain another L12 off it.
These are for plugging in a microphone (note: the L12 does NOT supply phantom power on these ports), or other balanced signal, for example, a feed from a mixing desk.
This input is for connecting an instrument (such as a guitar) to the L12, giving you access to the L12's built-in DSP effects.
This input is used for connecting a device at "line level" to the L12, examples include computers, electric pianos, and more.
This input is used for connecting laptops, smartphones, etc...
Please ensure this switch is set appropriate to the region you are operating the L12 at.  Options are 110V and 240V.  Incorrect setting can cause damage to the L12.
Turns the L12 on and off, please ensure that the "Voltage Selector" is set to the correct voltage before supplying power.
This is where you supply power to the L12, please ensure that the "Voltage Selector" is set to the correct voltage before supplying power.
